description_long: This object is a collection of mid-rise apartment buildings arranged in a grid-like formation, typical of an urban residential block. The buildings vary in size, color, and roof style, with some featuring flat roofs and others with pitched or gabled roofs. Facades are adorned with regularly spaced windows, and some buildings have visible balconies or small architectural details such as awnings and cornices. The color palette includes muted tones such as beige, gray, green, and brown, with occasional red or orange roofs. The structures are primarily constructed from concrete, brick, and glass, with metal elements likely present in window frames and railings. The overall appearance suggests a mix of early to mid-20th-century architectural influences, suitable for a dense city neighborhood.
description: A group of mid-rise apartment buildings with varied facades, muted colors, and a mix of flat and pitched roofs, arranged in a city block layout.
description_view_0: This view shows the apartment buildings from an angled perspective, highlighting the variety in roof shapes and the arrangement of the buildings in rows.
description_view_1: From this end-on view, the depth and layering of the buildings are visible, with taller structures at the back and smaller ones in front.
description_view_2: A side view reveals the linear alignment of the buildings and the diversity in facade treatments and window arrangements.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the separation between building clusters and provides a clear look at the rear and side elevations.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in regular, symmetrical rows and columns, with consistent spacing and alignment across each building facade.
